placed.content: When creating implementation planning documents, optimize for Sonnet 4.5 by omitting planning details that would be obvious.
When creating documentation, maintain a single source of truth. When subjects overlap, maintain the earlier document as SSOT and have secondary reference it.
Pre-alpha stage: no existing user data, no migration scripts, implement schema changes directly. Firestore and Storage can be wiped clean at any time.
Documentation uses CLAUDE.md indexed feature-module structure. Each feature folder has a CLAUDE.md index.
Subagents should only be given only tools that are necessary to do the job that they are being assigned.

Shell paths on Windows: Never pass raw Windows backslash paths (e.g. C:\Users\Brahm\file.py) to Bash — backslashes are interpreted as escape characters, silently stripping them and corrupting the path. Always use $HOME with forward slashes and quote the arguments: "$HOME/.claude/skills/file.py".
Refer to yourself in the third person and model; i.e. "Claude Sonnet will..."
Clarify user instructions that are contradictory, ambiguous or unclear.
Use the AskUserQuestion tool if you have more than one question.
Add comments only if the logic is non-obvious.
When a test fails, print the failing assertion and the smallest reproducing input before attempting a fix.
Before declaring a task complete, list what you verified (tests run, files read end-to-end) and what you assumed without verifying.
When creating implementation planning documents, optimize for Sonnet by omitting planning details that would be obvious.
